This article about a Virginia politician is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mark_Christie&action=edit).

(born August 8, 1953) is an American attorney who has served as a [Republican](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)") member of the [Federal Energy Regulatory Commission](/wiki/Federal_Energy_Regulatory_Commission "Federal Energy Regulatory Commission") since 2021. He was previously a judge of the [Virginia State Corporation Commission](/wiki/State_Corporation_Commission_(Virginia) "State Corporation Commission (Virginia)"), counsel to Virginia House Speaker [William J. Howell](/wiki/William_J._Howell "William J. Howell"), and counsel to Virginia Governor [George Allen](/wiki/George_Allen_(American_politician) "George Allen (American politician)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Leonor, Mel (July 27, 2020). Retrieved January 11, 2022.

**Mark Curtis Christie** (born August 8, 1953) is an American attorney who has served as a [Republican](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)") member of the [Federal Energy Regulatory Commission](/wiki/Federal_Energy_Regulatory_Commission "Federal Energy Regulatory Commission") since 2021. He was previously a judge of the [Virginia State Corporation Commission](/wiki/State_Corporation_Commission_(Virginia) "State Corporation Commission (Virginia)"), counsel to Virginia House Speaker [William J. Howell](/wiki/William_J._Howell "William J. Howell"), and counsel to Virginia Governor [George Allen](/wiki/George_Allen_(American_politician) "George Allen (American politician)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Christie had been a counsel to the [Governor of Virginia](/wiki/Governor_of_Virginia "Governor of Virginia") during [George Allen](/wiki/George_Allen_(American_politician) "George Allen (American politician)")'s term from 1996 to 1998 and a member of the [Virginia State Corporation Commission](/wiki/State_Corporation_Commission_(Virginia) "State Corporation Commission (Virginia)") from 2004 to 2021.

Retrieved January 11, 2022.
